entries.note: The feature was largely already in place from prior feature builds: AttentionBanner already reads ?attention=<id>, scrolls to the item, applies a 2s pulse via React state, and clears the URL param via setSearchParams. FeatureBoard already reads :itemId from useParams, scrolls via useLayoutEffect, and highlights via el.setAttribute. WorkflowDetailRoute already renders a 'Workflow not found' error page with back link after an 8s timeout when no snapshot arrives. What was missing: (1) subscription deduplication in the WS client — subscribe() now returns early if the workflow is already in this.subs, preventing duplicate subscribe frames (AC-7/RC-4); (2) e2e tests — created src/web/e2e/deep-link.spec.ts covering AC-2 (item deep link highlights card), AC-4 (invalid workflow shows error via Playwright clock.fastForward), AC-5 (in-app navigation via history.pushState+popstate triggers highlight), and AC-7 (subscribe frame sent exactly once). Also fixed a pre-existing strict locator in workflow-detail.spec.ts (FeatureBoard status filter test had two comboboxes with the same aria-label; narrowed to getByRole('main')). All 72 tests pass.
deferred_criteria: AC-1: ?attention scroll coverage is supplemented but not fully isolated in a new deep-link test (the existing workflow-detail.spec.ts test at line 434 covers it). No new standalone attention test was added in deep-link.spec.ts because it would duplicate coverage.
AC-3 (attention pulse): Covered by the existing test in workflow-detail.spec.ts; no additional test written. Item pulse is verified in AC-2 test via data-highlight attribute.
known_risks: FeatureBoard highlight via direct DOM mutation (el.setAttribute) is fragile: if a WS frame triggers a React re-render within the 2s window, the JSX prop data-highlight="false" will reconcile and overwrite the attribute.
page.clock.fastForward is used in AC-4 tests to skip the 8-second not-found timeout.
In-app navigation test (AC-5) uses history.pushState + manual popstate dispatch.
The subscribe deduplication fix returns early for ALL duplicate calls.

entries.note: Attempt 2 addressed the two deferred criteria from attempt 1 (AC-1 and AC-3 for attention deep-links). Three new standalone tests were added to deep-link.spec.ts: (1) AC-1 cold-start attention test — navigates to /workflow/:id?attention=42 and verifies the banner renders with the targeted item; (2) AC-3 attention pulse test — verifies data-highlight=true on the attention item; (3) AC-6 URL cleanup test — verifies the ?attention= param is removed from the address bar by history.replaceState. Additionally, the test infrastructure was repaired: helpers.ts had sessionStartedFrame phase required (breaking 1-arg callers in usage-hud.spec.ts) — fixed by making phase default to 'implement'. usageFrame() builder was added to helpers.ts. Two strict-mode locator violations introduced by UsageHUD (getByText('ok') matching 'Yoke'/'tokens', getByText('Test Workflow') matching sidebar row + detail header) were fixed in stream-pane.spec.ts and usage-hud.spec.ts. UsageHUD.tsx improvements from a prior session (dropdownRef outside-click fix, cache-write tokens, sessions.size > 0 threshold) were committed. All 130 tests pass.
known_risks: The attention deep-link pulse test (AC-3) checks data-highlight=true immediately after page load. The 2s setTimeout clearing the highlight could theoretically expire on a very slow machine before the assertion runs, but in practice the assertion runs well within 100ms of page load.
AC-1/AC-3 tests use ATTN_ID=42 (numeric). If the attention ID format ever changes to non-numeric, Number('abc')=NaN means the highlight would never apply but text assertions would still pass.
usage-hud.spec.ts AC-8 'outside click' test clicks on locator('main') at position {x:10, y:10}. If the portal position changes, the click might land inside the dropdown.
